questions.question_text: From the top of a 75-meter-high lighthouse, the angles of depression of two ships are $30^\circ$ and $45^\circ$. If the two ships are on the same side of the lighthouse and in a straight line with the base, and the distance between the two ships is (Use $\sqrt{3} = 1.732$),

A pentagon is divided into three triangles by drawing diagonals from one of its vertices. The areas of two of these triangles are $30 \text{ cm}^2$ and $25 \text{ cm}^2$. The third triangle has a base of 12 cm and a height of 30 cm. What is the area (in $\text{cm}^2$) of the pentagon?
The price of an article is successively increased by 9% and 13%. Find the percentage increase in the price of the article.

The present age of a father is equal to sum of ages of his son and daughter. Ten years ago, the ratio of age of the father and the daughter was 9:4. Ten years hence, the age of the daughter. The total present age of the father, daughter and son is ______ years.
A positive integer N divides 228, leaving a remainder of 18. What is the greatest two-digit value of N?

Pipes A and B, can fill a tank in 10 hours. Pipes B and C, can fill the same tank in 20 hours. If pipe C alone can fill the same tank in 30 hours, how much time will the pipes A and C take together to fill the same tank.

Chetan's salary is ₹16000 per month. He spends ₹6000 on house rent, ₹1500 on bills and the rest of the amount is his monthly savings. Find his savings(in ₹) in a year, if in the month of his birthday he spent his complete monthly saving for birthday celebration.
Simplify: $$\left[\frac{13}{10}\left(\frac{21}{13} \div \frac{5}{9} \times \frac{18}{15} \div \frac{7}{7}\right)+\frac{10}{6} \div \frac{20}{15} \text{ of } \frac{15}{6}\right]$$

A faster train overtakes a slower train, both moving in the same direction. The faster train takes 36 seconds to completely pass the slower train. A passenger sitting in the faster train observes that he passes the slower train in 12 seconds. Find the ratio of the length of the faster train to the length of the slower train.

Two trains, 130 meters and 120 meters long, are running in parallel tracks in the same direction at speeds of 68 km/hr and 44 km/hr respectively. How much time will the faster train take to completely cross the slower one?
If the average of four numbers is 15, and three of the numbers are 10, 20, and 25, what is the fourth number?
In $\triangle ABC$, $\angle A = (3x-7)^\circ$, $\angle B = 2\angle A$ and $\angle C = (4x+6)^\circ$. Find the value of $\angle A + \angle C$.
The simple interest on a certain sum for 4 years at 10% interest per annum is ₹3,200. What will be the amount payable after 6 years at the same rate?

A and B can complete a piece of work in 12 days and 8 days respectively. Both work together for 2 days and then A leaves. B completes the remaining work alone. In how many days is the whole work completed?
₹5,278 is divided among X, Y, and Z in such a way that if ₹67, ₹61, and ₹68 are deducted from their respective shares, they have money in the ratio $8:20:5$. Find X's share after deduction.
Surjeet buys 20 kg of sugar for ₹900. He sells 18 kg of sugar for ₹918. What is the percentage profit made by Surjeet?

A trader buys a mobile phone for ₹8000, spends ₹500 on repairs, and sells it at a profit of 25% on the total cost. Later, the buyer resells it at a loss of 25%. What price does the final buyer pay for the mobile phone?
Simplify: $\sqrt{8^2 + \sqrt{8^2 - 49 - 6}}$
Ravi buys an article with a discount of 25% on its marked price. He makes a profit of 10% by selling it at ₹1,320. The marked price of the article was:
Three numbers are in the ratio $3:7:2$, and their LCM is 5376. Their HCF is:
What sum (in ₹) will earn a simple interest of ₹1,320 in 4 years at 8% per year rate of interest?

A store offers a 10% festival discount on the marked price of ₹900. Later, to clear the stock, it gives an additional 5% off on the already discounted price. What is the total amount paid by the customer?
The ratio of the radii of two spheres is $2:3$. If the product of their LCM and HCF is 294, then find the sum of the two numbers.
The numerator of a fraction is 8 less than the denominator. If the denominator is increased by 5 and the numerator is increased by 3, we get the fraction $\frac{3}{4}$. Find the original fraction.

In a class, the average weight of the boys is 45 kg and the average weight of the girls is 50 kg. The average weight of the whole class is 47 kg. What is the ratio of the number of girls to the number of boys in the class?
Find the third proportional to 8 and 48.2.

Two friends, P and Q, started a business by investing ₹3,000 and ₹600, respectively. At the end of one year, the business earned a total profit of ₹54,846. What is the share (in ₹) of P in the profit?
Two positive numbers are in the ratio $2:3$. If the product of their LCM and HCF is 294, then find the sum of the two numbers.
$324 \times 24 = 7776$. What will $7.776 \div 2.4$ be equal to?

In the production of crystal sugar from sugarcane juice, the sugar is separated from the liquid through the growth of solid particles (sugar crystals) from a concentrated solution (syrup). This separation technique is called _____.

options.text: To promote literature in Indian languages

questions.question_text: In which of the following ways has the private sector contributed to India's industrial growth and global expansion since the economic reforms of 1991?
What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? FIN KNS PSX UXC ?

Seven people F, G, H, I, J, L, M, O and Q are sitting in a row facing north. Only five people sit between H and O. Only three people sit between L and Q. Only one person sits between H and L. Q sits immediately to the left of I. How many people sit between L and O?
In a certain code language, 'BIKE' is coded as '4821' and 'KITE' is coded as '2146'. What is the code for 'G' in the given code language?

Based on the alphabetical order, three of the following four are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group? (Note: The odd man out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

Refer to the following letter series and answer the question that follows. Counting to be done from left to right only. (L) R E X Z T M E D F T B S I K A Q Y O W G U (Right) How many such vowels are there in the given arrangement that is immediately preceded by a consonant and also immediately followed by a consonant?

Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s). Statements: (i) All frogs are watches. No logs are wings. Conclusion (II): Some wings are ants.
All 245 people are standing in a row facing north. Cod is 24th from the right end while Yes is 26th from the left end. How many people are there between Cod and Yes?
The position(s) of how many letters will remain unchanged if each letter in the word SERENITY is arranged in the alphabetical order?

(Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster pairs.)

Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s). Statements: (I) All frogs are wings. No logs are wings. Conclusion (II): Some wings are ants.

E.g. 13 => Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) 18, 306, 17 21, 389

(Left) 6 4 9 2 8 4 2 9 8 1 6 7 2 5 8 7 3 2 5 4 (Right) How many such even digits are there, each of which is immediately preceded by an even digit and also immediately followed by an odd digit?

Select the pair which follows the same pattern as that followed by the two pairs given below. Both pairs follow the same pattern. DDZ-ADA LIJ-FIF
X is the mother of F. F is the sister of T. U is the father of K. K is the son of T. How is X related to U?

Mr. Vasu starts from Point A and drives 25 km towards the north. He then takes a left turn, drives 17 km, takes a right turn and drives 28 km, takes a right turn and drives 10 km, takes a left turn and drives 30 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 29 km. He takes a final left turn and drives 18 km to stops at Point B. Find the shortest distance and the direction in which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90-degree turns unless specified.)
If 2 is added to each odd digit and 1 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 163742. How many digits will appear more than once in the new number thus formed?
